evals.expected_output: Runs the validation script against ./datasets/, produces a markdown report with all four check categories, and presents a pass/fail summary with the PFS fidelity table.

evals.prompt: I just regenerated the data with n=200 — can you check the new CSVs look right before I package the ZIP?
evals.expected_output: Runs validation, reports per-category results, flags any cross-domain mismatches or structural violations, and confirms whether PFS medians are within tolerance of the published 10.3 vs 5.1 month targets.

evals.prompt: something looks off with the response rates in the RS domain — can you audit the RECIST derivation?
evals.expected_output: Focuses on Category 3 (RECIST Derivation) results — checks that RS RSSTRESC values match the −30%/+20% thresholds derived from TR TRSTRESN, and reports any classification mismatches.
